context: Tests whether the agent implements the piecewise confidence formula with correct calibration constants for DJL face_feature cosine distance, avoids the textbook linear formula, and correctly separates the threshold-based identity decision from the continuous confidence measure.

checklist.name: Piecewise lower bound
checklist.description: confidenceOf returns 1.0 for d <= 0.30 (strong match flat region)

checklist.name: Piecewise upper bound
checklist.description: confidenceOf returns 0.0 for d >= 0.65 (reject boundary is 0.65, not 0.60)

checklist.name: Piecewise linear region
checklist.description: confidenceOf uses (0.65 - d) / 0.35 for the middle region (divisor is 0.35)

checklist.name: No textbook formula
checklist.description: Does NOT use the pattern `1 - d / TOL` or `1f - dist / 0.6f` (or any variant with a single tolerance divisor) for the confidence calculation

checklist.name: Identity threshold 0.60
checklist.description: identityLabel uses 0.60 as the threshold to decide 'unknown' vs a named label (discrete identity decision)

checklist.name: Threshold vs confidence separation
checklist.description: The identity threshold (0.60) and the confidence function (confidenceOf) are implemented as separate mechanisms — not collapsed into one formula

checklist.name: Cosine distance formula
checklist.description: cosineDistance returns 1 - dot(a, b), treating the inputs as L2-normalized (d = 1 - dot product, not 1 - magnitude ratio)

checklist.name: Correct rejects upper bound in comments
checklist.description: Code or comments explain the 0.65 upper bound specifically for DJL face_feature (not 0.60 from dlib-based calibration)

checklist.name: Calibration explanation
checklist.description: Comments or documentation mention that the linear formula compresses strong matches (d=0.20-0.30) into the middle band

checklist.name: Correct cosine distance semantics
checklist.description: Code treats lower cosine distance as a stronger match (not higher), i.e., finds the minimum distance match in identityLabel
